seats.answer:
A merge gate combines multiple incoming flows (parallel or alternative) into one outgoing flow in a workflow/process model. 

Final answer: combines multiple flows into one (e.g. in BPMN/workflows)
A merge gate ensures changes meet required checks and policies before they are merged.
The purpose of a merge gate is to smoothly combine multiple incoming pathways—such as conveyor lines, traffic, or data—into a single, organized outgoing stream without collisions.
A merge gate is a quality control checkpoint that ensures code changes pass all automated tests, security checks, and peer reviews before they can be merged into a target branch.
